title: Renaissance Venice in the 1400s
topic_path: Northern Italy: Venice, Ferrara, and the Marches

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following is **NOT** correct?

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a72bf27d221e3592bfc040e49493c6d.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Bellini was a master of oil paint (not tempera). [Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/giovanni-bellini-san-zaccaria-altarpiece-1505).

choices.content: The title is the San Zaccaria Altarpiece
This was made using tempera paint
This painting is indebted to the Byzantine tradition, seen in the mosaics in the Basilica of San Marco.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The portrait busts of Roman emperors that surround the oculus in Mantegna's *Camera degli Sposi* are particularly appropriate to Mantua because . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca40d79dc7f57938145bec5dec19ffd2.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In the 1460s, Mantegna was the court painter to Duke Ludovico Gonzaga, the ruler of the city of Mantua.
[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/andrea-mantegna-camera-degli-sposi-frescos-in-the-ducal-palace-mantua-1465-74)

choices.content: the Italian city-state of Mantua was ruled by a marquis.
the citizens of Mantua were interested in Roman emperors because they reflected Republican ideals.
Mantua had reverted to pagan worship.
the Duke of Mantua was called emperor by his subjects.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the speakers, which building most likely influenced the architecture depicted in Bellini's *San Giobbe Altarpiece*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0bfa23a0546811a6b3c4ee113b48a1ea.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The mosaic that we see in the apse behind the figures is borrowed from Byzantine architecture, like that seen in the Basilica of San Marco in Venice. 
[Watch this video for Review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/bellini-san-giobbe-altarpiece)

assessment_items.question_content:
**A *sacra conversazione*, or sacred conversation, is a painting that shows the Madonna and Child talking to saints from different time periods.**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0bfa23a0546811a6b3c4ee113b48a1ea.jpeg)

Giovanni Bellini, *San Giobbe Altarpiece*, c. 1485, oil on panel, 471 cm × 258 cm / 185 in × 102 in (Accademia, Venice)

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Bellini's *St. Francis* reflects the Renaissance humanist idea that . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b299e5e99ed48559e36d65ef2511a136.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This painting reflects the Renaissance Humanist idea that natural beauty is infused with divinity. [Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/giovanni-bellini-saint-francis-in-the-desert-circa-1480)

choices.content: nature is infused with both good and evil
God and the angels are not real.
the landscape is a stepping stone to heaven

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Mantegna's *Dead Christ*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e5200d63891a8890ce7b62c0fabf0b9e.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are problems with the way that Mantegna foreshortened the body of Christ.
[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/mantegna-dead-christ-c-1490)

choices.content: The artist perfectly executed the foreshortening of the body of Christ.
This painting is a prime example of foreshortening, which draws the viewer by making the viewer feel present in the room with the dead Christ.
This painting of the dead Christ emphasizes Christ's humanity, which is consistent with Renaissance Humanism.
This painting was in Mantegne's possession at his death and may have been rejected by a patron because of its extreme view of Christ.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which elements of Mantegna's *San Zeno Altarpiece* identify the court of heaven as a classical building?**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f434b67f661dd14046053456359d2cc.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The relief sculpture, fluted columns, garlands, and sculpted frieze all point to classical architecture. 
[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/andrea-mantegna-san-zeno-altarpiece)

choices.content: the relief sculpture in the roundels
the fluted columns that are part of the frame

assessment_items.question_content:
**What characteristics of oil paint did Giovanni Bellini exploit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In Venice, oil paint was applied in thin translucent layers called glazes and dried very slowly. It also allowed for subtle blending of colors, which was not true of tempera. 
[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/oil-paint-in-venice)

choices.content: Oil paint can be applied in thin translucent layers
Oil paint allows for subtle blending of colors

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the speakers, Mantegna paid particular attention to drapery folds, which were inspired by $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/be89ca4a9124c5365f0f12b582ca5e5f.jpeg)
Andrea Mantegna, *Dormition (or Death) of the Virgin*, c. 1462, tempera on panel, 
54 × 42 cm, 21.26 × 16.54 in (Museo del Prado, Madrid)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Mantegna paid particular attention to the drapery folds, which were inspired by classical sculpture.
[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/renaissance-venice/venice-early-ren/v/mantegna-dormition-or-death-of-the-virgin-c-1462)
